chunks.page_summary: This page describes how to connect the Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME) to a local area network using Modbus TCP over Ethernet to access sensor data, illustrating a typical LAN setup.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Connecting the Modbus TCP outputs

Modified on: Fri, 12 Sep, 2025 at 4:28 PM

1. Overview

The Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME) (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/) offers the Modbus TCP (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217062) service over the Ethernet (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000219061) interface. To begin using Rheonics Input Registers (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297664) for accessing sensor data, the first step is to connect the SME to the local area network (LAN).

2. Wiring

The SME can be connected to a network using its Ethernet RJ45 connector (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000300292), with intermediary devices such as routers or switches. Figure 1 illustrates a typical LAN setup, where multiple devices, including the SME, are interconnected via a switch.

Figure 1. SME as part of an Ethernet Network

Another option is to directly connect the SME to an Ethernet host, as shown on Figure 2.
key_facts: The Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME) provides Modbus TCP service over an Ethernet interface.
The initial step to access sensor data using Rheonics Input Registers is to connect the SME to a local area network (LAN).
The SME connects to a network via its Ethernet RJ45 connector.
Intermediary devices like routers or switches are used for network connection.
Figure 1 depicts a typical LAN setup where multiple devices, including the SME, are interconnected through a switch.
An alternative connection method involves directly connecting the SME to an Ethernet host (illustrated in Figure 2, not shown on this page).
diagram_labels: Figure 1. SME as part of an Ethernet Network

chunks.page_summary: This page describes the MODBUS TCP/IP connection of a Rheonics SME device to an Ethernet host, provides default network configuration parameters, and directs users to resources for detailed setup instructions.
chunks.extracted_text:
MODBUS Server TCP/IP
MODBUS Client TCP/IP
Figure 2. Direct connection between SME and Ethernet Host

3. Configuration
The Rheonics SME device's Ethernet IP address, subnet mask, gateway, and DNS can be configured using the RCP software (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000211411). Please refer to the RCP software manual for instructions on setting these parameters and Rheonics Support Portal (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions) for SME network configuration via USB (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/articles/81000411282).
The factory default values are shown on Table 1.

Table 1. Default Network Settings
Configuration Parameter | Value
DHCP | TRUE
IP Address | 0.0.0.0
Mask | 0.0.0.0
Gateway | 0.0.0.0
DNS | 0.0.0.0
TCP Port | 502
Slave ID | 255

The default TCP Port, and slave ID can't be modified by the user.
key_facts: The Rheonics SME device supports MODBUS TCP/IP communication.
The SME device's Ethernet IP address, subnet mask, gateway, and DNS are configurable.
Configuration of network parameters for the SME device is performed using RCP software.
Detailed instructions for RCP software and SME network configuration via USB are available on the Rheonics Support Portal.
The factory default network settings include DHCP set to TRUE, IP Address, Mask, Gateway, and DNS all set to 0.0.0.0, TCP Port 502, and Slave ID 255.
The default TCP Port and Slave ID are not user-modifiable.
warnings_notes: The default TCP Port, and slave ID can't be modified by the user.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview and configuration details for the 'Communication' tab within the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) software, covering Modbus RTU, Ethernet, LCD display settings, location ID, Bluetooth, and operational history for Rheonics inline viscometers and density sensors.
chunks.extracted_text:
When DHCP is enabled (set to TRUE), a DHCP server, typically the LAN router, will automatically assign an IP address to the Sensor SME electronics. The assigned IP address can be viewed in the RCP (Rheonics Control Panel) under the "Communication" tab in the Ethernet section.

[Screenshot content follows, parsed into labels and values below.]

Figure 3. RCP Communication Tab

The IP address assigned to the SME (whether static or dynamic), along with the input registers (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297459), is used by the Modbus Client to access measurement data and other diagnostic values from the sensor.

If you encounter any issues with Ethernet or Modbus, please refer to our Modbus TCP/IP Troubleshooting Guide (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000397718) available on the Rheonics Support Portal (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions).
key_facts: When DHCP is enabled (set to TRUE), a DHCP server (typically the LAN router) will automatically assign an IP address to the Sensor SME electronics.
The assigned IP address for the Sensor SME electronics can be viewed in the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) under the "Communication" tab in the Ethernet section.
The IP address of the SME (static or dynamic) and its input registers are used by a Modbus Client to access measurement and diagnostic data from the sensor.
Rheonics provides a 'Modbus TCP/IP Troubleshooting Guide' and a 'Rheonics Support Portal' for assistance with Ethernet or Modbus issues.
warnings_notes: If you encounter any issues with Ethernet or Modbus, please refer to our Modbus TCP/IP Troubleshooting Guide available on the Rheonics Support Portal.
